title: Astronaut Truth Stack
summary: Astronauts train for years to operate complex systems in extreme environments while performing science, maintenance, and high-stakes decision-making.
starterPack: Start with NASA astronaut selection and ISS operations.
Read Chris Hadfield and Cady Coleman for lived perspective.
Use NASA and ESA video channels for mission reality.

tiers.title: First Principles
tiers.description: The physical and operational constraints behind astronaut work.

items.name: Spaceflight is constrained by physics
items.description: Orbital mechanics, life support, and mass limits shape every decision.

items.name: Procedure is safety
items.description: Training, redundancy, and discipline keep crews alive.

items.name: Astronauts are operators and scientists
items.description: The role mixes experimentation, maintenance, and mission support.
